text.title: Research Repo Naming — Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes when applying the convention. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: PII in filename: a real name or email leaks via the filename, becomes visible in screenshots, sync logs, and search indexes.
failure-mode.detector: regex match for `[A-Z][a-z]+` (capitalised word) at the participant slot OR `@.*\.` (email pattern) anywhere in filename
failure-mode.repair: Stop. Rename via the participant-id map. Rotate the leaked artefact (replace with anonymised copy). If the original was synced to cloud storage, audit history for exposure.

failure-mode.description: Filename collision: two artefacts get the same name (often `notes.md`, `transcript.vtt`) in different folders, breaking flat indexes.
failure-mode.detector: scripts/lint produces COLLISION for identical basenames across the tree
failure-mode.repair: Re-name per grammar; the filename should be unique by construction. Add a descriptor segment if needed.

failure-mode.description: Lost language: mixed UK/EN repo, files lack `.<lang>.` suffix; RAG retrieves wrong-language quotes for synthesis.
failure-mode.detector: repo has files with `lang == auto-detect-uk` AND files with `lang == auto-detect-en` AND any filename lacks the lang suffix
failure-mode.repair: Run `scripts/extract_metadata_from_transcript` to auto-detect, then rename. Backfill from the body language; if uncertain, escalate to manual.

failure-mode.description: Synthesis without source citations: `synth/2026-05-themes.md` is created with no frontmatter pointing to the raw files. Cannot audit which quotes drove the theme.
failure-mode.detector: file in `synth/` with frontmatter missing `sources: [...]` OR sources contain filenames not in manifest
failure-mode.repair: Block publication of the synthesis. Re-author with explicit source list. Synthesis is only credible when its sources are auditable.

failure-mode.description: Drifting study-type tags: researcher uses `int` for some interviews and `interview` or `conversation` for others.
failure-mode.detector: manifest.type_counts contains any key not in the closed enum
failure-mode.repair: Mass-rename to the canonical tag. Update lint script to fail-closed when a non-enum type appears.

failure-mode.description: Participant renumbering: P001 becomes P008 after a "cleanup" pass; published synthesis citations break.
failure-mode.detector: participants/map.json has entries where original_id != current_id AND any synth/ file references original_id
failure-mode.repair: Revert. Renumbering after publication is forbidden by r3 enforcement. New participants get the next free P###.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Filenames are sometimes constructed by an LLM tagger (the researcher pastes a transcript and asks for a filename). The tagger MUST refuse to emit a filename that contains real names found in the body, even if asked. Anonymisation is not optional and is the user's downstream legal exposure.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Every rename and re-numbering operation runs through `scripts/lint-research-repo.py` before commit. The lint script is the human's external memory; the researcher reviews any violation rather than auto-fix.
